p.seg: To all sons of Holy Mother Church, present and future, Philip of Pitcox sends greeting.

Know me to have given and granted, and by this my charter to have made firm, out of the good will and agreement of Philip my son and heir, to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose, and to the monks in that place serving God, for the souls of my lords, Cospatric, and Waldef, earls of Dunbar, and for the souls of my father and mother, and of all my ancestors, and for the salvation of my lord, Patrick, earl of Dunbar, and for my own salvation, and of Philip my son and heir, and of all my successors, the land in the territory of Pitcox which lies between the boundaries of the monks' land at Hartside and the little stream that is called Pressmennan Burn, which the aforesaid monks have annexed along the aforesaid stream to their own land at Hartside by reason of my donation and instruction; and ten acres in ploughland at 'Beleside' most closely adjacent to their land at Edmundstoun; in free, pure, and perpetual alms; as freely and quitely, fully and honourably, as they freely, quitely, fully and honourably hold and possess any alms. And I and my heirs shall maintain and warrant the said land to the aforementioned monks against all men for ever, and we shall discharge claims in respect of the lord king and all others from every service and custom and secular exaction.

As witnesses: Earl Patrick my lord; Patrick his son; Bernard Fraser; Roger de Merlay; Adam of Polwarth; Adam of Gordon; Thomas of Nisbet; Philip my son and heir; Waldef parson of Whittinghame; Master William of Earlston; Master John, parson; and many others.
